dab.def: 輕拍、塗抹、少量、熟手的人的人人物 (單數) (拍/熟)
dab.col: dab of paint, dab at eyes
dab.ex: She used a small dab of paint for edge.

dabble.def: 涉獵、淺嘗、戲水的人的人
dabble.col: dabble in politics, dabble in art
dabble.ex: He likes to dabble in local politics.

dabbler.def: 涉獵者、淺嘗輒止者人物 (單數) (淺)
dabbler.ex: He is a mere dabbler in the arts.

dace.def: 雅羅魚、石斑魚人物 (單數) (魚)
dace.ex: The silver dace swam in the stream.

dacha.def: (俄)別墅、鄉間小屋人物 (單數) (屋)
dacha.ex: They spent summer at their local dacha.

dactyl.ex: The poem uses a metrical dactyl flow.

dactylic.def: 揚短短格的人的人物 (詩)
dactylic.ex: The epic was written in dactylic form.

dad.ex: Hi dad, can you help me with this box?

daddy.ex: The small girl ran to her daddy now.

dado.def: 護牆板、牆裙、底座的人物、設護牆板的人的人人物 (單數) (牆)
dado.ex: The room has a beautiful oak dado.

daemon.def: (古)半神、守護神、(計)後台程序人物 (單數) (神/程)
daemon.ex: The system daemon handles network flow.

daffodil.def: 水仙花、黃水仙人物 (單數) (花)
daffodil.ex: Bright daffodils bloom in early spring.

daffy.ex: He has a very daffy and funny laugh.

daft.ex: That was a really daft thing to do.

dagger.def: 匕首、短劍、劍號的人物、刺的人的人人物 (單數) (匕)
dagger.col: look daggers at (憤怒注視), cloak and dagger (秘密情報)
dagger.ex: She looked daggers at him across room.

dahlia.def: (植)大麗花、天竺牡丹人物 (單數) (花)
dahlia.ex: A red dahlia bloomed in the garden.

daily.def: 每日的、日常的、日報、女傭的人的人人物 (單數) (日)
daily.col: daily routine, daily bread, on a daily basis
daily.ex: Exercise is part of my daily routine.

dainty.def: 精巧的、美味的、優雅的人的人物 (單數) (精)
dainty.col: dainty feet, dainty morsel (美味一小塊)
dainty.ex: She took a dainty bite of the cake.

dairy.def: 乳製品、乳牛場、乳製品店的人物 (單數) (乳)
dairy.col: dairy product, dairy farm, dairy free
dairy.ex: Milk and cheese are common dairy foods.

dairying.def: 製酪業、乳業人物 (單數) (乳)
dairying.ex: He is studying modern dairying now.

dairyman.def: 乳品商、牧場工人人物 (單數) (乳)
dairyman.ex: The local dairyman delivered the milk.

dais.def: 講臺、高臺、主座人物 (單數) (臺)
dais.ex: The king sat on a high raised dais.

daisy.def: 雛菊、(口)極好的人或物、最好的人的人人物 (單數) (菊)
daisy.col: pushing up the daisies (已死), fresh as a daisy
daisy.ex: She felt as fresh as a daisy today.

dale.ex: They walked through the green dale.

dally.def: 閒混、嬉戲、耽擱的人的人
dally.ex: Don't dally or we'll be late for work.

dalliance.def: 調情、閒混、玩弄的人物 (單數) (混)
dalliance.ex: It was just a brief and silly dalliance.

dam.def: 水壩、大壩、築壩的人的人人物 (單數) (壩)
dam.col: build a dam, dam up (堵住)
dam.ex: The dam provides power for the city.

damage.def: 損害、損失、賠償金、損害的人的人人物 (單數) (損)
damage.col: cause damage, severe damage, structural damage
damage.ex: The storm caused severe local damage.

damaged.def: 受損的、損壞的人的人 (或物) (損)
damaged.ex: The car was badly damaged in the crash.

damages.def: 賠償金(法)人物 (複數使用) (賠)
damages.ex: He sued the company for heavy damages.

damaging.def: 破壞性的、有害的人 (或物) (害)
damaging.ex: The report had a very damaging effect.

damask.def: 大馬士革緞、碎色、(由)大馬士革緞造的人物、粉紅色的人的人物 (粉)
damask.ex: She wore a dress of beautiful damask.

dame.def: 夫人、女爵、(口)女人人物 (單數) (爵)
dame.ex: Dame Judi Dench is a famous actress.

damn.def: 詛咒、指責、(口)一點點的人的人物、極端地的人的人人物 (單數) (罵)
damn.col: damn it, not worth a damn, damn with faint praise
damn.ex: I don't give a damn about his opinion.

damnable.ex: This rain is a real damnable nuisance.

damnation.def: 詛咒、入地獄的人物 (單數) (罰)

damned.def: 該死的、受詛咒的人的人 (或物) (死)
damned.col: damned if I know, damned lie
damned.ex: That is a whole damned local lie now.

damp.def: 潮濕的、濕氣、挫傷、弄濕的人的人人物 (單數) (濕)
damp.ex: The walls feel very damp and cold now.

dampen.def: 弄濕、抑制、減弱的人的人
dampen.ex: The bad news manage to dampen spirits.

damper.def: 阻尼器、抑止者、減震器人物 (單數) (阻)
damper.ex: The rain put a damper on the party.

dampish.ex: The grass was still a bit dampish now.

dampness.ex: The general dampness made me cough.

damsel.def: 少女、小娘子人物 (單數) (女)
damsel.ex: The knight saved the damsel in distress.

damson.def: 西洋李子、深紫色人物 (單數) (李)
damson.ex: She made some sweet purple damson jam.

dance.def: 舞蹈、舞會、跳舞、跳躍的人的人人物 (單數) (舞)
dance.col: first dance, dance to someone's tune, tea dance
dance.ex: May I have the honor of this dance?

dancer.def: 舞者、舞蹈家人物 (單數) (舞)
dancer.ex: She wants to be a professional dancer.

dancing.ex: They spent the whole night dancing now.

dandelion.ex: The yellow dandelion grew in the path.

dandified.def: 花花公子樣的、服裝時髦地的人的人 (或物) (時)
dandified.ex: He gave us a very smart dandified look.

dandify.def: 使成花花公子樣、扮時髦的人的人
dandify.ex: They tried to dandify the local boy.

dandle.def: 寵愛、(在膝上)搖弄的人的人
dandle.ex: He began to dandle the baby on his knee.

dandruff.ex: He suffers from a lot of scalp dandruff.

dandy.def: 時髦的人、花花公子、極好的的人物、時髦的人的人人物 (單數) (時)
dandy.ex: Everything is just fine and dandy here.

danger.def: 危險、威脅、危險事物人物 (單數) (危)
danger.col: in danger, out of danger, clear and present danger
danger.ex: The firemen were in great local danger.

dangerous.ex: It's a very highly dangerous mountain.

dangerously.ex: He always lived dangerously in past.

dangle.def: 懸掛、吊著、誘惑的人的人人物 (單數) (懸)
dangle.ex: Keys began to dangle from her belt.

dank.def: 陰暗潮濕的、透心涼地 (冷)
dank.ex: They were kept in a dark and dank cell.
